data.title: Critical Metals stock soars 45% — but it isn't too late to invest yet
data.description:
Shares of New York-based Critical Metals Corp ripped higher on April 17 after the company announced a major regulatory breakthrough and received positive analyst coverage.

As investors cheered both updates, CRML broke decisively above its major moving averages (50-day and 100-day), signalling shifting momentum in favour of the bulls.

Despite its sharp rally on Friday, Critical Metals stock remains down some 35% versus its year-to-date high.

What’s driving Critical Metals stock higher today?

CRML shares are charging up this morning primarily because Greenland has approved the transfer of a 50.5% interest in the Tanbreez rare-earth project to the Nasdaq-listed firm.

This increases the company’s total stake to a commanding 92.5%, granting it nearly total strategic and operational autonomy over one of the planet’s premier heavy rare-earth deposits.

For investors, this isn’t just a paperwork victory; it’s a massive de-risking event.

In the world of mining, jurisdictional hurdles often prove the “valley of death” for junior explorers.

By clearing this regulatory bar, Critical Metals has secured its grip on a resource that is increasingly vital for the production of permanent magnets used in electric vehicle motors and defence tech.

In short, it cements CRML as a primary beneficiary of the West’s “de-risking” strategy – aimed at ending the dependency on overseas supply chains.

Is there any further upside left in CRML shares?

While Critical Metals shares have already doubled in April, Texas Capital analysts remain bullish, seeing the rare earth miner hitting $20, signalling potential upside of another 50% from here.

According to them, the New York-headquartered firm is uniquely positioned to fill a widening gap in the global market for critical minerals.

Beyond Tanbreez, which boasts high recovery rates and a favourable mineralogical profile, Texas Capital is bullish on the company’s recent $1.5 billion joint venture with Saudi conglomerate TQB as well.

Plus, the investment firm highlighted CRML as the primary beneficiary of the US-led “friend-shoring” initiative.

Critical Metals has secured a $120 million Letter of Intent from the Export-Import Bank of the United States.

As Western nations scramble to decouple from Chinese rare earth dominance, Tanbreez (located in Greenland, a NATO-aligned jurisdiction) is seen as a strategic asset of national importance rather than just a commercial mine.

What’s next for Critical Metals Corp?

CRML stock is now heading into a “catalyst-rich” summer, with the commissioning of a pilot plant scheduled for May 2026.

As the global race for resource sovereignty intensifies, Critical Metals finds itself in the right place at the right time.

For those looking beyond daily “noise,” the combination of regulatory certainty, institutional backing, and a strategic asset makes this more than just a momentum trade.

CRML has, in fact, evolved into a play on the very foundation of the modern energy transition.

data.title: Germany's Wacker Chemie sees Q1 core profit above market estimates
data.description:
Overview

  • Germany specialty chemicals maker expects Q1 EBITDA to exceed market expectations on order pull-forward

  • Q1 sales seen at €1.41 bln; full-year EBITDA guidance unchanged

  • Company raises full-year sales growth outlook to high single digits as higher costs passed to customers

Outlook

  • Wacker Chemie expects Q1 2026 EBITDA of about €173 mln, above prior company guidance and market view

  • Company maintains full-year 2026 EBITDA guidance at €550 mln to €700 mln

  • Wacker Chemie raises full-year 2026 sales growth outlook to high single-digit percentage range

Result Drivers

  • ORDER PULL-FORWARD - Co said Q1 EBITDA was boosted by customer orders brought forward due to the conflict in the Middle East

Company press release:

Key Details

Metric

Beat/Miss

Actual

Consensus Estimate

Q1 Prelim Revenue

EUR 1.41 bln

Q1 Prelim EBITDA

EUR 173 mln

Analyst Coverage

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is 5 "strong buy" or "buy", 10 "hold" and 4 "sell" or "strong sell"

  • The average consensus recommendation for the specialty chemicals peer group is "buy."

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Wacker Chemie AG is €76.00, about 17.8% below its April 16 closing price of €92.50

  • The stock recently traded at 127 times the next 12-month earnings vs. a P/E of 92 three months ago

For questions concerning the data in this report, contact Estimates.Support@lseg.com. For any other questions or feedback, contact reuters.support@thomsonreuters.com.

data.title: WaFd reports Q2 net income $65.5M, diluted EPS $0.82; ROA 0.96% and tangible ROE 10.8%
data.description:
WaFd reported quarterly net income of $65,548,000 for the quarter ended March 31, 2026, with diluted earnings per common share of $0.82. The company posted a net interest margin of 2.81% and return on average assets of 0.96%; return on tangible common equity was 10.82%. Total assets were $27.57 billion and tangible common shareholders' equity per share grew to $30.27.

Financial Highlights

  • Net income: $65,548,000 for the quarter ended March 31, 2026 (up 2% from prior quarter, up 17% year-over-year).
  • Diluted earnings per common share: $0.82 for the quarter ended March 31, 2026 (compared to $0.79 prior quarter; $0.65 year-ago quarter).
  • Net interest income: $177,570,000 for the quarter; net interest margin: 2.81%.
  • Return metrics: Return on average assets 0.96%; Return on tangible common equity 10.82%.
  • Total assets: $27,568,785,000; tangible common shareholders' equity per share: $30.27.

Business Highlights

  • Loan activity: Grew active loan segments with originations of $1.5 billion in the quarter; commercial loans represented 96% of originations.
  • Balance sheet positioning: Investment securities increased $919 million over six months, driven by $1.2 billion of purchases (primarily discount-priced 30-year MBS with expected YTM of 4.87%).
  • Deposits and funding mix: Customer deposits totaled $21.1 billion with transaction accounts representing 60.3% of deposits; time deposits declined over the period.
  • Asset quality trends: Non-performing assets declined to $132 million (0.48% of total assets); delinquencies were 0.78% of total loans and net charge-offs were $0.6 million for the quarter.
  • Shareholder returns: Repurchased 2,738,096 shares during the quarter at a weighted average price of $31.85; board authorized up to 10 million total repurchase shares (8.0 million remaining authorization).

Original SEC Filing: WAFD INC [ WAFD ] - 8-K - Apr. 17, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: SRx Health Solutions files S-4, EMJX merger update shows $18M deployment and positive early returns
data.description:
SRx Health Solutions issued an update on its proposed merger with EMJ Crypto Technologies and reported $18 million deployed to its Gen2 treasury strategy delivering a 5.69% return in March 2026.

Key Highlights:

  • Filed Form S-4 for the proposed merger with EMJ Crypto Technologies to register shares for the transaction.
  • Deployed $18 million into EMJX Gen2 digital-asset treasury strategy, producing a 5.69% return in March 2026.
  • EMJX outperformed S&P 500 by 10.67% in March 2026 amid market declines, demonstrating hedging efficacy.
  • Through mid-April, hedging pool and long treasury holdings posted ~3.5% and ~8% gains, respectively.
  • Upon closing, EMJX will operate as a wholly-owned subsidiary and founder Eric M. Jackson to join as an officer.

Original SEC Filing: SRx Health Solutions, Inc. [ SRXH ] - 8-K - Apr. 17, 2026

data.title: Think the rally is safe? This elite market-timing indicator says a correction is overdue.
data.description:
By Mark Hulbert

The gold-platinum ratio suggests the stock market is living on borrowed time

The U.S. stock market's prospects continue to deteriorate, according to the gold-platinum ratio - a relatively obscure indicator with an impressive track record predicting the stock market's 12-month return.

I say "continue" because the ratio of gold (GC00) to platinum (PL00) was already declining when I last wrote about it in November. As you can see from the accompanying chart, the ratio has declined since then. That doesn't bode well for the S&P 500 SPX.

The market-timing potential of the gold-platinum ratio was first reported in the Journal of Financial Economics in a study entitled "Gold, platinum and expected stock returns." The authors found that the ratio "outperforms nearly all existing return predictors" when predicting the stock market's return over the subsequent 12 months.

The researchers based this conclusion after analyzing data from 1975 to 2013. The accompanying chart, reflecting data since 2014, constitutes an out-of-sample test of their conclusion. Though the ratio isn't a perfect predictor of the stock market's subsequent 12-month return, it has done a creditable job since 2014. It did particularly well navigating the COVID-19-induced bear market in 2020 and subsequent recovery, for example.

The reasons that the gold-platinum ratio is a good market-timing indicator, according to the study, are that the two metals respond to different factors. While both are correlated with the economy, gold - but not platinum - also reflects geopolitical risk and uncertainty. A rising ratio means that geopolitical risk is growing, and when that happens, stock investors will demand a higher expected return to compensate them for that risk. That causes the stock market to fall as the ratio is rising, but to have higher returns in subsequent months.

To put the researchers' argument another way, as a coincident indicator, the ratio is inversely correlated with the stock market. But as a leading indicator, it is positively correlated.

You may have a hard time believing that the gold-platinum ratio is falling because geopolitical risk is receding. But this narrative is not as far-fetched as it otherwise might appear. Consider a geopolitical risk index (GPR) created by Federal Reserve economists Dario Caldara and Matteo Iacoviello. It's a news-based index that measures "the threat, realization and escalation of adverse events associated with wars, terrorism and any tensions among states and political actors that affect the peaceful course of international relations."

Though the GPR is quite volatile on a day-to-day basis, in mid-April it dipped to less than half of its early-March high - and less than a third of its 2025 high last June.

Note that the gold-platinum ratio sheds little light on the stock market's performance over the next month or two. So even if the market is lower in 12 months, the ratio tells us nothing about the path it took to get there. But it's hard to overlook the ratio's decline began almost 12 months ago, which suggests that the stock market may be living on borrowed time.

Mark Hulbert is a regular contributor to MarketWatch. His Hulbert Ratings tracks investment newsletters that pay a flat fee to be audited. He can be reached at mark@hulbertratings.com

More: Former Treasury Secretary Henry Paulson warns U.S. needs an emergency 'break-the-glass' plan if Treasury demand collapses

Plus: Tesla, IBM and Intel report earnings next week. Here's the best way to play a volatile market.

-Mark Hulbert

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Oil Selloff May Be Overdone — Market Talk
data.description: Oil futures sink as Iran says the Strait of Hormuz is open to all shipping for the duration of the cease-fire and President Trump reiterates that a deal is expected soon. But even with the strait open, "the physical market is not going to be able to correct as quickly as what the market is showing," says BOK Financial's Dennis Kissler. "We've still got a logistics problem that's going to take at least 30 days to correct, to get the oil half-way in transit to where it needs to go." The market does need to move a little lower, "but the front-month futures are overdoing the break from where the physicals are going to be." WTI for May delivery is off 12% at $83.01 a barrel and June Brent is down 11% at $88.82.(anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

data.title: Nymex Overview: Petroleum Futures Ease Off Early Losses But Still Sharply Lower — OPIS
data.description:
Crude oil and refined product futures prices were still seeing deep losses heading into Friday afternoon even as prices eased off lows seen earlier in the morning.

The sharp declines come as markets react to Iran reopening the Strait of Hormuz Friday in response to a 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon.

U.S. oil prices were down nearly 13% at about 11:20 a.m. ET, with the May NYMEX West Texas Intermediate crude contract down $11.81 to $82.88/bbl after falling as low as $80.65/bbl earlier in the morning. The front-month contract is moving toward expiration early next week, so most activity is focused on the June contract, which was down $10.04 to $81.13/bbl.

June Brent crude was $10.53 lower to $88.86/bbl while July prices were down $8.18 to $85.39/bbl. Brent contracts are trading more than $2.50 above earlier lows.

Most activity in refined product futures was also focused on the next-month contract.

ULSD futures were trading 11.8% lower, with May prices down 44.96cts to $3.3833/gal and June prices off by 37.49cts to$3.2713/gal. Earlier in the morning the front-month contract had fallen as low as $3.2603/gal.

RBOB futures were seeing prices about 7% lower, with the front-month contract down 21.18cts to $2.9519/gal after falling as low as $2.8906/gal. June prices were down 20.60/gal to $2.8853/gal.

If the price declines hold, crude and ULSD futures will end the week at the lowest levels in more than a month.

The Iranian announcement Friday said the strait would remain open for commercial traffic during the Israel and Lebanon ceasefire. President Trump on Friday, however, said the United States would continue its own blockade of the strait that began Monday and targets shipping approaching or leaving Iran.

Trump, however, also expressed confidence that a deal would soon be reached to end the fighting that began Feb. 28, when the United States and Israel launched strikes on Iran.

The drop in futures prices was sending gasoline and diesel prices sharply lower in spot markets around the country. Equity markets were seeing strong gains heading into Friday afternoon, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average up more than 2% and the NASDAQ Composite rising by 1.7%.

- -

This content was created by Oil Price Information Service, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. OPIS is run independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

Reporting by Steve Cronin, scronin@opisnet.com; Editing by Donna Harris, dharris@opisnet.com

(c) 2026 Oil Price Information Service, LLC. All rights reserved.

data.title: Albemarle Had Its Best Day Since 2023. The Lithium Stock Gets Downgraded Anyway. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Mackenzie Tatananni

Albemarle stock received a downgrade Friday, one day after shares of the lithium miner jumped 16% to notch their highest close in well more than two years.

Baird analysts cut their rating on Albemarle to Neutral from Outperform while keeping their price target unchanged at $120. Albemarle stock fell 8.3% to $197.74 on Friday. Shares ended Thursday's session at $215.62, marking their highest level since July 21, 2023.

Even before then, the lithium miner had been a relative outperformer. Heading into Friday's session, Albemarle had risen more than 52% in 2026 against a 2.9% gain for the benchmark S&P 500. Baird attributes the most recent strength to upbeat commentary from Chinese battery maker Contemporary Amperex Technology (CATL), which reported earnings earlier this weak.

Reports of a temporary truce between Israel and Lebanon, which were later confirmed, also may have contributed to the stock move Thursday. Baird noted that "peace in the region would de-risk ALB's Specialties operations nearby."

So what prompted the retreat to the sidelines? While an improving demand backdrop would be a positive catalyst for shares, "we fear that focus will now shift to global supply and higher-cost sources may once again come online and limit pricing upside," the analysts wrote.

China's Sichuan Yahua Industrial Group announced this week that it had secured a six-month export quota for lithium concentrates from Zimbabwe, just two months after the African country banned exports of raw lithium.

The development signals potential regulatory easing and a partial resumption of production for outside the country, Baird wrote. "While full exports are not expected to resume until 2027, this is an early data point of additional supply coming back on-line," analysts added.

Speaking of CATL, the battery maker's fourth-quarter earnings report and strong outlook are regarded as positives for both demand and lithium pricing. However, future price increases "will likely incentivize additional supply coming online and tempter pricing upside to an extent, " in Baird's view.

Albemarle's valuation could be another source of pushback for investors. The miner was trading at around 25 times forward earnings as of Thursday's close, versus 21 times for the S&P 500.

The multiple is slightly ahead of peers, Baird noted. "With uncertainty regarding the supply picture looking ahead, we speculate investors may be less willing to ascribe a higher multiple even in light of an improved demand backdrop," analysts wrote.

Write to Mackenzie Tatananni at mackenzie.tatananni@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Nevermiss Launches AI Receptionist to Help Small Businesses Capture Leads and Boost Revenue
data.description:
JACKSONVILLE, FL / ACCESS Newswire / April 17, 2026 / In the modern service economy, the speed of response is often the primary determinant of a business's commercial success. For small businesses-particularly in sectors such as home services, healthcare, and professional consulting-every missed call represents a tangible loss in revenue and potential long-term growth. To address the critical issue of unanswered customer inquiries, Nevermiss today announced the launch of its advanced AI-powered receptionist platform, designed to ensure that no call goes unanswered, regardless of the time of day or the volume of incoming requests.

Research indicates that a significant percentage of service-oriented businesses struggle to maintain constant availability due to staffing constraints, after-hours demands, and the inherent unpredictability of incoming lead flow. When a potential customer reaches a standard voicemail box, the friction created often leads them to contact a competitor immediately. Nevermiss serves as a strategic infrastructure layer for small businesses, providing an automated yet personalized interface that captures leads, answers frequently asked questions, and manages appointment scheduling with human-like precision. By maintaining a constant digital presence, the system prevents the revenue leakage typically associated with administrative downtime.

"Small business owners are currently facing a paradox where they must grow their customer base while simultaneously struggling to manage the basic communication channels that facilitate that growth," said a spokesperson for Nevermiss. "Hiring additional front-desk staff is often cost-prohibitive for smaller operations, and traditional voicemail systems have become obsolete in an era that demands instantaneous engagement. Nevermiss was built specifically to bridge this gap, ensuring that businesses can capture, qualify, and convert every caller into a booked job without the overhead associated with traditional human resource expansion."

The platform utilizes sophisticated conversational AI to handle a wide range of administrative tasks that typically consume the time of business owners. By integrating directly with scheduling calendars and CRM systems, the software identifies the caller's intent in real-time, provides accurate information regarding services, and secures booking appointments before the interaction concludes. This capability is particularly vital for service businesses that experience high call volumes during off-peak hours or weekends, times when traditional office staff are unavailable. Through the Nevermiss dashboard, owners gain visibility into their communication logs, ensuring total oversight of lead management and customer interactions.

Beyond the immediate benefit of booking jobs, the system functions as a robust lead-capture tool. By gathering essential contact information and documenting customer inquiries, the platform ensures that no lead is lost to the digital abyss of an ignored voicemail. The data collected during these interactions allows business owners to analyze their lead pipeline more effectively and allocate resources toward the most profitable service segments. For those interested in seeing how this technology impacts the bottom line, detailed insights can be found at the company's official website.

The scalability offered by this AI-driven solution is another key advantage for growing enterprises. Unlike human personnel, who are subject to the constraints of physical capacity and burnout, the AI platform can handle dozens of concurrent inquiries without a degradation in service quality. This allows service providers to expand their service areas and marketing efforts with the confidence that their intake capacity can evolve alongside their business. Detailed information regarding these capabilities is accessible via the Nevermiss support resources.

As the competitive landscape for local services continues to tighten, the ability to provide 24/7 responsiveness is shifting from a premium amenity to a mandatory operational standard. Nevermiss is positioned to help independent operators reclaim the revenue that is traditionally lost to the ‘voicemail gap.' By leveraging machine learning to deliver a seamless client experience, the platform enables business owners to focus on their core expertise-delivering high-quality services-while the technology handles the complexities of client intake.

The platform is available for immediate integration, providing a turnkey solution for service businesses looking to modernize their customer support operations and improve their bottom line. For more information regarding Nevermiss and its suite of administrative tools, visit the Nevermiss landing page.

About Nevermiss

Nevermiss is a leading provider of AI-driven communication solutions tailored for small and medium-sized service businesses. The company specializes in building intelligent receptionist technology that automates lead capture, appointment booking, and customer query management. Headquartered in San Francisco, Nevermiss is dedicated to empowering local businesses with enterprise-grade operational efficiency.

Media Contact

Public Relations Department

media@nevermiss.com

https://nevermiss.com/

SOURCE: Nevermiss

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: Cruise Stocks Get Lift From Opening of the Strait of Hormuz — Market Talk
data.description: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East has only had a modest impact on cruise demand, UBS analysts say in a research note, citing a recent conversation they had with one of the largest U.S. cruise sellers. That seller noted during the conversation, though, that he knows not all sellers are as optimistic as him. As it stands, results over the past 30 to 40 days have been a mixed bag, with cruises to the Caribbean "performing extremely well" and Alaskan cruises benefiting from a shift in demand. Eastern Mediterranean is seeing some hesitation, and cruise lines as a result have upped promotions, the analysts say. The promotions are non-cash value adds--such as airline point offers--rather than simple price decreases, they add. Cruise stocks are up Friday after Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi declared the Strait of Hormuz open. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

data.title: Strategist flags froth but finds opportunity outside megacaps
data.description:
Key points:
  • Main US indexes advance; Dow out front, up >2%
  • Cons Disc leads S&P 500 sector gainers; Just Energy, Utilities down
  • Euro STOXX 600 index up ~1.5%
  • Dollar down; US crude plunges >12%; gold up >1%; bitcoin up >3%
  • US 10-year Treasury yield falls to ~4.24%

STRATEGIST FLAGS FROTH BUT FINDS OPPORTUNITY OUTSIDE MEGACAPS

Since its late-March trough, the market's repricing has been striking, going from oversold to overbought in no time.

"We are enamored by this environment. We are among the few who like Treasuries despite persistent bond market pressure driven by fears about this administration," writes Mike O'Rourke, chief market strategist at JonesTrading, in a note out late Thursday.

In his view, "Risk assets represent bubble valuations and bubble behavior. We hate the S&P 500 at the index level due to its being historically expensive. Simultaneously, we love why it is so expensive."

According to O'Rourke, the crowding into megacap leadership has crowded out the rest of the market, compressing valuations of other companies. Therefore, as he sees it, this is one of the most compelling non-megacap single-stock environments he can recall. In short, while O'Rourke "hates" the market, he believes numerous individual stocks can be identified as attractive investments.

In any event, O'Rourke says he can't help but notice that froth is percolating under the surface as the most active groups outside the megacaps this week have been quantum names and nuclear stocks.

Quantum stocks are surging because Nvidia released an AI model. However, O'Rourke views the nuclear story as "lunar lunacy," given that the U.S. government wants to put small modular reactors on the moon by 2030.

"At least theoretically, the reason for investing in companies is to invest in profitable businesses," he writes. But as he sees it, it's hard to imagine that being a profitable venture, and it's even harder to envision the United States government executing.

O'Rourke adds, "No one has brought more seemingly impossible scenarios to reality than Elon Musk, but his projects consistently take significantly more time and money than anticipated and are generally accompanied by considerable volatility."
